name: Biddeford-Saco Country Club
about: Biddeford-Saco Country Club is a premier golf destination in Maine featuring an 18-hole Donald Ross-designed course and extensive practice facilities. The venue has operated since the early 1920s and has evolved into a member-owned club maintaining affordable rates and competitive play opportunities. The facility offers a comprehensive golf experience with professional instruction, group lessons, and TrackMan 4 technology for winter training, along with amenities for public play, private events, and dining overlooking the course.
why_golfers_like_it: One of the best ranges and practice facilities in the state
Affordable membership rates with full capacity
Host to numerous state championships and tournaments
Proximity to Old Orchard Beach vacation destination

food_and_drink.notes: Clubhouse dining room available for private functions

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Players should bring their own golf equipment to enjoy the full course experience, though equipment rentals may be available upon request.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The clubhouse dining room is available for private functions and offers food and beverage service, but external food and drink policies may vary.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Standard 18-hole play time is typical of a full round, though specific duration may vary based on course conditions and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Biddeford-Saco Country Club offers junior memberships and is home to the State Champion Thornton Academy Golf Team, making it suitable for youth golfers.
